creator: tech-lists@zyxst.net
text:
(In reply to Gerard Seibert from comment #198)

My system was affected by the bug; it's been "fixed" [1] for a while, with my chipset at least, on this motherboard.

The affected system runs stable/13-n245706-526441c080e with GENERIC kernel.
It seems the usb3 controller card is no longer detected by freebsd:

$ pciconf -lv |grep -i usb
    device     = 'C600/X79 series chipset USB2 Enhanced Host Controller'
    subclass   = USB
    device     = 'C600/X79 series chipset USB2 Enhanced Host Controller'
    subclass   = USB

$ cat /usr/src/sys/amd64/conf/GENERIC | grep xhci
device		xhci			# XHCI PCI->USB interface (USB 3.0)

It was detected as 'ASM1142 USB 3.1 Host Controller'

Personally, i'm happy that this seemingly problematic card first threw errors and then was eventually undetectable (and therefore unusable). It means that if i really need a working usb3 then i'll slot in a different card known to work. The worst is when something errors undetected.

[1] the way I "fixed" it before was to just build a kernel without xhci. But now with or witout xhci in the kernel, this hardware is undetected.
